question: Will real GDP increase by more than 2.0% in Q1 2026?
reasoning: The Atlanta Fed GDPNow model has consistently revised Q1 2026 real GDP growth estimates downward throughout March and April 2026, falling from over 3% in early March to just 1.3% as of April 7th. This latest estimate of 1.3% is well below the 2.0% threshold needed for the market to resolve YES. While the New York Fed nowcast is higher at 2.4%, the Atlanta Fed model is specifically designed to track GDP using the same methodology as the BEA's advance estimate that will determine the market resolution.
key_factors: Atlanta Fed GDPNow latest estimate of 1.3% for Q1 2026 (as of April 7)
Consistent downward revisions from 3.1% in February to 1.3% in early April
Weak retail sales and personal consumption expenditure data driving revisions lower
Q4 2025 GDP was revised down to just 0.7% (annualized), showing economic momentum slowing
Market resolves on April 30th BEA advance estimate, just 3 weeks away
risks: New York Fed nowcast at 2.4% suggests some models see higher growth potential
Final economic data releases before April 30th could surprise to upside
GDPNow model has average absolute error of 0.77 percentage points historically
Strong labor market data (unemployment at 4.3%) could support consumer spending
AI-related investment strength mentioned in some forecasts could boost growth
